Revenue.broad: crm-oct-pipeline: October pipeline close analysis. 3 deals pushed from October to November/December: Glenbrook Financial (ARR $45,000, close moved to Nov 15 — procurement delay), Axis Media Group (ARR $28,000, close moved to Dec 1 — procurement delay), Summit Retail Corp (ARR $9,000, close moved to Nov 30 — champion changed, deal re-qualified). One deal lost: Clearwater Tech ($12,000 ARR) to competitor Apex AI on price. Total miss: $82,000.
Revenue.narrow: crm-oct-deals-detail: Glenbrook — 95% probability, procurement sign-off pending, CRO contact engaged daily. Axis — 80% probability, new procurement lead responsive. Summit — 60% probability after champion change, re-demo scheduled Nov 20. Total slipped pipeline in firm forecast: $73,000. Clearwater loss is permanent.

Professional Services.broad: crm-ps-oct: Professional Services revenue from SOW projects. Two SOW renewals (Glenbrook $3,500, Axis $2,500) held pending primary deal close — will be booked when revenue deals sign. One new PS engagement delayed due to onboarding dependency on Summit close.
Professional Services.narrow: crm-ps-detail: PS revenue tied directly to software deal close timing. Glenbrook and Axis PS SOWs are signed but not yet invoiced — will hit November/December with primary deals.

Cost of Revenue.broad: crm-cor-oct: Cost of Revenue analysis for October. $12,000 underspend reflects lower hosting and support costs from reduced new customer onboardings — two enterprise onboardings (Glenbrook, Axis) pushed to November with their deals. Variable COGS scales with revenue recognition.
Cost of Revenue.narrow: crm-cor-detail: COR variance is directly linked to revenue miss — hosting and onboarding costs for Glenbrook ($7,000) and Axis ($5,000) not yet incurred. Will be recognised when deals close.

Revenue.broad: sales-slack-2024-10-31: October close recap — we missed plan by $82K. Three slips and one loss. Glenbrook procurement just needs a signature. Axis new procurement lead is engaged. Summit is a re-qualify risk. Clearwater went to Apex on price — nothing more we could do. November plan needs to absorb the three slips.
Revenue.narrow: sales-slack-2024-10-31: CRO comment: November is achievable if Glenbrook and Axis close on time. Summit is upside. We've adjusted November forecast accordingly. Board pack will show October miss with context.

Professional Services.broad: ps-slack-2024-10-31: PS miss is entirely downstream of software deals. Glenbrook and Axis PS SOWs are ready to execute but we can't invoice until the primary deals close. This will all normalise in November.
Professional Services.narrow: ps-slack-2024-10-31: No PS deals were lost. All $6,000 shortfall is deal-timing dependent, not pipeline risk.
